input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

1. Check if .NET 9 and .NET 10 SDKs are available

data.content: Let me fix the runtime specification - BenchmarkDotNet may not have Core100 yet. I'll use RuntimeMoniker instead:
data.reasoningText:
The BenchmarkDotNet version doesn't have `CoreRuntime.Core100` yet. Let me check what's available or use a different approach - I can use the moniker-based approach with `SimpleJob` attributes instead.

Actually, since BenchmarkDotNet multi-targets both frameworks via the project, I can let it just run the default job for each TFM. Let me simplify the approach - I'll just use the `[SimpleJob]` attribute with the RuntimeMoniker enum, or better yet, just rely on the multi-targeting and run once for each framework.

Let me check what RuntimeMonikers are available in BDN 0.14.

data.content: The BDN version doesn't support .NET 10 monikers yet. Let me simplify - I'll run benchmarks separately for each framework:
data.reasoningText:
The BenchmarkDotNet version available doesn't support .NET 10. Let me check what version of BenchmarkDotNet is resolved, and check the available RuntimeMoniker values.

Actually let me just take a simpler approach - run the benchmarks separately for each target framework without specifying a runtime in the attributes. Each run will use its own framework.

data.reasoningText:
Wait, the earlier attempt with `RuntimeMoniker.Net100` failed because the BDN version doesn't have that. Let me check what version of BDN is being used and what monikers are available.

Actually wait - it compiled and ran successfully with Net90, so the issue was specifically Net100. Let me try a different approach: I'll just remove the RuntimeMoniker attribute entirely and run separately for each framework. The host runtime determines what the benchmark runs on.
